progression.title: West to east: Area 05, the border, Vostok

official.afterLinkHtml: , the world is laid out as three regions. You start in Area 05, an evacuated part of southeastern Finland: shelters, traders, tasks, and early loot, while Bandits patrol the zone. When you are geared enough, the road continues east into the Border Zone—guarded crossings between Finland and Russia—then, if you cross, into Vostok in Russia (high risk; permadeath—dying there costs your whole run stash, unlike the western zones).

sandbox.beforeLinksHtml: The game is still a sandbox: you are not forced into Vostok or a single playstyle. “Pushing east” simply means following that intended difficulty curve—secure the west, then attempt harder crossings—not a hidden story mandate. For step-by-step locations use

inline.beforeStartHere: ; for controls and the survival loop,

area05.title: Area 05
area05.text: Hub shelters, traders, tasks, starter gear. Hostile bandits in the evacuation zone.

border.title: Border Zone
border.text: Guarded border maps: mines, obstacles, boats, and corrupt Guards with air support.

vostok.title: Vostok
vostok.text: Best loot, Military faction, armored threats. Every Vostok map is permadeath.

demo.heading: Public demo: how the journal lines up
demo.lead: The playable demo is only a slice of that world, but it usually nudges you through the same idea: learn your shelter, clear Area 05–style work, follow traders and keys, then the UI may show a completion line often labeled “All Tasks.”

flow.ariaLabel: Typical demo order: shelter, Area 05, traders and keys, then All Tasks journal state
flow.captionHtml: “All Tasks” means the main task list for that demo build is finished. It is not a promise that Early Access or later builds will have nothing left to do.

quest.zonesAriaLabel: Three regions from west to east

about.title: About this Road To Vostok guide
about.text1: Road To Vostok (Road To Vostok Ltd.) is a hardcore single-player survival FPS on the Finland–Russia border. You loot in Area 05, cross the Border Zone, and can enter Vostok under stricter permadeath rules than the rest of a run. The free Steam demo and the Early Access release drive most searches: how to play, loadout and medical questions, tasks, roadmap timing, and PC saves.
about.text2: This English-language guide covers those topics with practical articles on loadouts, the long-term roadmap, crafting, and fishing as patches add them — always double-check what your installed build actually shows.

releaseEa.q: What is the Road To Vostok release date and Early Access plan?
releaseEa.a: Steam lists a planned Early Access window (check the store page for the current date). The in-game and site roadmap guides summarize build phases after that launch.

multiplayer.q: Is Road To Vostok online or multiplayer?
multiplayer.a: No. The Steam store describes it as a single-player survival game. There is no live co-op or MMO mode in that description.

demoSteam.q: How do I get the Road To Vostok demo on Steam?
demoSteam.a: Use the Steam store entry for Road To Vostok and install the separate demo app (Steam shows a demo download when available).

whatFirst.q: How to play Road To Vostok — what should I do first?
whatFirst.a: Run any tutorial map the demo offers, unlock your shelter save point, then learn looting, traders, and how to load weapons before pushing toward harder maps.

loadWeapons.q: Road To Vostok: how to load shotgun and how to load weapons?
loadWeapons.a: Magazine guns need loaded mags in your inventory; shotguns and bolt actions use manual per-round steps shown in the tutorial. See our Load weapon guide for a written checklist.

inverter.q: Where is the Road To Vostok inverter or power gear?
inverter.a: Use the inverter location guide: search industrial and utility POIs, pair with trader hints, and verify spawns in your patch.

fracture.q: I died from a broken bone in Road To Vostok — what now?
fracture.a: Treat fractures fast with splints or advanced kits before movement penalties and passive damage stack. The medical guide walks through afflictions and healing items.

allTasks.q: How do I complete Road To Vostok all tasks?
allTasks.a: Follow the quest chain on the homepage, then the Tasks page for objectives and trader keys and the Map for POIs.

roadmap.q: Where is the Road To Vostok roadmap?
roadmap.a: Open the roadmap guide and the Road ahead hub for build names, Early Access scope, and future systems like fishing and seasons as announced.

mods.q: Are there Road To Vostok mods?
mods.a: The Steam Early Access FAQ states modding tools are planned toward full release. Demo builds may not expose full mod support yet.

backup.q: How to backup Road To Vostok saves on PC?
backup.a: Paths vary by build and OS. Snapshot your user data folder before experiments; the Mods hub explains layout at a high level — always verify on your machine.

crafting.q: How does crafting work in Road To Vostok?
crafting.a: Crafting expands across Early Access builds. Check patch notes and in-game recipes; the Wiki tracks loot that feeds crafting loops.

fishing.q: Is fishing in Road To Vostok?
fishing.a: Fishing and naval content appear on the official multi-year roadmap for later builds — not always in every demo slice.

officialSite.q: Is this an official Road To Vostok website?
officialSite.a: No. This is an independent reference site. Cross-check facts with your installed build and official channels.

lootTable.sectionLeadHtml: Community-maintained item stats from RTV Tools (same fields as their loot table). Icons load from that host. Filter by rarity, source pool, trader letter, and item type — then sort. Always verify values in your installed Road To Vostok build.
lootTable.sectionAria: Loot table with search and filters
lootTable.footnoteHtml:
VPS = sell value ÷ inventory slots the item occupies.

Stack value = value × default stack for stackables (ceiling when a full stack sits in one slot).

VPS (weighted) = VPS × rarity proxy: Common ×1.00, Rare ×0.20, Legendary ×0.01, Null ×0 (no container drop expectation).

Source tags: CIV civilian loot pools, MIL military, IND industrial ground loot. Trader tags: G Generalist, D Doctor, S Gunsmith, M Grandma.

mapPage.title: Road To Vostok Map
mapPage.introHtml: Use the map for Area 05, the Border Zone, and Vostok, then read the regional overview and Area 05 notes below. For trader tasks, border crossings, and Vostok rules, open the Tasks page. Verify against your installed build.
mapPage.hintHtml: Pins are locations only (Village, Shipyard, …). Each place can list NPCs, loot, tasks in the left panel and in the index below — those are not separate markers. After you select a pin or location row, the details drawer slides in from the left; close with × or reopen with Details. Sub-items with a wiki link jump to that page. Pins are approximate.
mapPage.villageDetailCalloutHtml: Village detail map: For a closer layout of the Village play space with multiple pins and notes on our own raster (same Leaflet tool as the overview), open Village detail map. Pin positions are approximate — align to your screenshot and in-game checks.

mapPage.pinSearchHasMatches: {n} matching — press Enter to jump to the next.
mapPage.regionalSectionHtml:
Regional overview

The official site describes a linear world progression: you start in Area 05 (Finland), push east through the Border Zone, and may eventually enter Vostok in Russia — with different death consequences in each tier.

Area 05

An evacuated zone in southeastern Finland: shelters, traders, tasks, and starting loot for early survival. Hostile Bandits patrol the area.

Border Zone

A guarded frontier between Finland and Russia. Crossings differ: mines, obstacles, or waterways with boats. Guards are armed, corrupt, and may call air support.

Vostok

High-risk, high-reward Russian territory with the most valuable loot. Every Vostok map is a permadeath map. The Military faction fields heavy weapons and armored vehicles.
mapPage.area05DetailProseHtml:
Area 05 map guide & where loot comes from

According to the official Game page, Area 05 is your starting region: an evacuated zone in the southeastern part of Finland where you will find shelters, traders, tasks, and starting loot for initial survival. Your routine there is interrupted by hostile Bandits exploiting the lack of authority.

Loot and “spawn points” in practice

The same page lists physics-based loot as a core feature: items exist as physical objects in the world rather than abstract menu pickups. The developers do not publish a public table of fixed item coordinates on roadtovostok.com, and many systems are described as dynamic (events, weather, replayable runs). So this page does not list guaranteed refresh coordinates — those change with patches and procedural layout.

  • Scavenge logically: search the kinds of places the setting implies (settlements, roadsides, abandoned structures) and learn your current build’s loot density in-game.
  • Use traders & tasks: the official feature list includes trading and tasks as part of the core loop alongside shelters — barter and job rewards are intended progression paths, not only ground spawns.
  • Stay alive to keep loot: if you die in Area 05, the official Vostok section states you only lose what you are carrying — not your whole progression context (that harsher rule applies to death inside Vostok).

On this site: Wiki (gear tables), Start here (loop, controls, medical basics), Border geography guide, Minefield guide, Tasks & Vostok rules.
mapPage.area05MapImgAlt: Area 05 — atmospheric map reference art alongside the guide

mapVillagePage.title: Road To Vostok — Village Map — Area 05 detail

mapVillagePage.hintHtml: Use the filter panel on the left of the map to show or hide marker types; the numbers show how many markers of each type appear on this map. Click the map or a row in the list below to open details in the popup. After opening a marker, you can bookmark or share the page from your browser to return to the same spot. Scroll or pinch to zoom; drag to pan.

mapVillagePage.railLeadHtml: Toggle categories or individual types. Numbers show how many markers of each type appear on this map.
mapVillagePage.footnoteHtml: Note: Independent player guide — not affiliated with the developer. Trademarks belong to their owners. Always confirm marker positions and loot in your installed Road To Vostok build.
mapVillagePage.belowMapHtml:
Village map guide for Road To Vostok (Area 05)

Road To Vostok places you in a harsh survival loop through Finland toward Vostok; official marketing describes Area 05 as an evacuated Finnish zone with shelters, traders, tasks, and starting loot, interrupted by hostile bandits. This page is a third-party editorial layout: an interactive map with category filters (locations, enemies, quests, loot, NPC) so you can plan routes, scavenging stops, and trader visits next to our regional overview map.

How to read the pins

  • Markers sit on the art as approximate references — small shifts are normal after patches; align with your in-game view when in doubt.
  • Loot categories (crates, cabinets, electronics, and similar) illustrate types of places to search; they are not a guarantee of specific item rolls.
  • After you open a marker, use your browser’s bookmark or share on the page address so friends can jump to the same popup.

Accuracy & updates

Topics such as Road To Vostok village map, Area 05 layout, or trader and shelter positions should always be checked against your installed build and official sources. Dynamic systems, events, and procedural layout can move spawns; this guide exists to support orientation, not to replace the in-game map.

More on this guide site

For border crossings and Vostok rules, read Tasks & progression; for controls, medical basics, and the core loop, start with Getting started; for tables of weapons, ammo, and items, browse the Wiki.

tasksPage.title: Road To Vostok Tasks & progression
tasksPage.introHtml: Trader tasks, border crossings, and Vostok permadeath rules — aligned with the public roadtovostok.com game page. For the interactive map and POI index, open Map. Verify details in your build.
tasksPage.bodyAllTasksHtml:
All tasks & progression (what exists today vs roadmap)

The public game page advertises trading & tasks as a headline feature: you work with traders, complete jobs they offer, and use rewards to prepare for harder maps. That is the honest scope of “tasks” the developer markets for the survival loop leading eastward.

The Roadmap on the same site schedules deeper narrative systems later: Build 3 — Signal (target Q4 / 2026) adds a quest system and lore elements, and Build 8 — Silent Night (date TBA) lists a full questline. So searches for Road To Vostok all tasks should distinguish: trader tasks and sandbox objectives in current / Early Access builds versus a single finished story questline that the roadmap still places in a future build.

Practical order players follow

  1. Secure a shelter save loop in Area 05 and learn trader locations (official text ties shelters, traders, and tasks together in the starting region).
  2. Complete tasks that fund meds, ammo, and travel gear; stash surplus at the shelter before risky trips.
  3. When ready, commit to a Border Zone crossing that matches your kit (mines vs boats vs obstacles — official crossing variety). For mine-heavy routes, see the guide Surviving the Minefield.
  4. Treat Vostok as optional endgame: permadeath rules apply only there per the official description — you are not forced into that playstyle for every run.

Related: Core task items, Border geography, Minefield tactics, Map, Road ahead (builds), All guides.
tasksPage.bodyBorderVostokHtml:
Border Zone & Vostok crossing guide

Once you have enough equipment, the official copy says your route continues east into the Border Zone — a guarded border between Finland and Russia. Each Border Zone map is said to have a crossing point with different challenges: some use mines or physical obstacles, others require waterways and boats.

Guards and combat pressure

The site names Guards as foreign border forces who are armed, corrupt, and may rely on air support to stop crossings. Plan stamina, cover, and medical supplies accordingly — see Start here (weapons & loading) and the weapons table for combat prep.

After the border: Vostok rules

If you cross successfully, you enter Vostok (Russia). Official text stresses:

  • High risk, high reward — the most valuable loot in the game.
  • Permadeath on Vostok maps: dying in Area 05 or the Border Zone costs only what you carry; dying in Vostok costs everything (per the Game page).
  • Military control: heavy weapons and armored vehicles; their stated goal is to push you back.

The same page notes the game does not force you into the permadeath zone — you can stay in the sandbox elsewhere. Dynamic systems and randomized events are cited as replay drivers, with no single winning condition.

Early Access launch is listed on the official roadmap as April 7, 2026; map count and faction behavior will evolve — re-read patch notes after updates.

devUpdatesPage.title: Road To Vostok — Dev updates & public roadmap
devUpdatesPage.heroLeadHtml: Steam Early Access (Build 1 — Road) went live on April 7, 2026. What follows is still the developer’s public build plan from roadtovostok.com/game — same names and target windows we use on this guide. Patch reality always wins: read Steam news for the live changelog, and treat this page as an editorial briefing, not a mirror of every hotfix.
devUpdatesPage.snapshotEaNoteHtml: Official roadmap lists this date as the Early Access storefront release for Build 1 — Road on Steam (AppID 1963610). EA is an evolving branch — not a final “1.0.” Prefer Steam announcements over any third-party summary when a build slips or a feature ships quietly.
devUpdatesPage.snapshotFullNoteHtml: The public roadmap runs through Build 8 — Silent Night (full questline, world options, new faction) with a TBA date. There is no separate official “full version ships on …” line yet — expect scope to evolve during Early Access.
devUpdatesPage.liveChangesIntroHtml: Patch notes and surprise fixes almost always land in first-party channels first. This page does not mirror every hotfix — use these when you want the real changelog. The free demo remains on Steam as a separate AppID (2141300), linked from the main store page, if you want to compare behaviour without owning EA.
devUpdatesPage.followSteamTail: — usual home for patch posts and announcements.
devUpdatesPage.followSiteRestHtml: — game overview and the public Roadmap block (Build 1–8) we summarize below. Cross-check dates here if Steam wording shifts after an update.
devUpdatesPage.followVideoTail: — great for intent and direction; not always 1:1 with the build number in your local client.
devUpdatesPage.liveChangesCrossLinksHtml: On this site: Guides (geography, Minefield) · Map and Tasks · Start here
devUpdatesPage.guideSiteSectionTitle: Built before EA, updated for the launch window
devUpdatesPage.guideSiteSectionIntroHtml: Road To Vostok Guide (this site) was prepared ahead of the April 2026 Early Access date. Wiki tables, map pins, and task notes are editorial references — they can lag a patch. After every Steam update, spot-check loot, traders, and mechanics in your own install; our disclaimers on database pages apply here too.
devUpdatesPage.plannedBuildsIntroAfterHtml: . Build 1 shipped into Early Access on April 7, 2026; later build windows remain targets, not guarantees — watch Steam when a milestone slips or splits.
devUpdatesPage.footnoteHtml: Editorial summary only. If a date here disagrees with Steam or roadtovostok.com, trust the first-party source. Page revised April 2026 for the Early Access launch window; roadmap rows still match the official game page as of that pass.

gettingStartedPage.title: Road To Vostok — How to play & what to do first (PC & Steam demo)
gettingStartedPage.introHtml: Core loop on PC, default-style controls (verify in your build), loading shotguns and other firearms (this page, weapon loading), survival and medical when you fracture or bleed (medical section below), and crafting context (Wiki, Gathering). Facts follow the public Steam listing and community write-ups — always confirm in your installed demo or Early Access build.
gettingStartedPage.sectionCoreHtml:
How to play — core loop

On the official Steam store listing for the game, the developer describes Road To Vostok as a hardcore single-player survival game in a post-apocalyptic border region between Finland and Russia: you survive, loot, plan, and prepare to reach Vostok, where mistakes can end a run (including permadeath rules for that zone as described there). On this site, pair the overview with Tasks, Map, and NPCs when you want locations and trader context.

  • Area 05: shelters, traders, tasks, and starter loot — often the first sandbox before pushing east.
  • Border Zone: guarded crossings with varied hazards (mines, obstacles, boats on some routes, hostile guards with air support — per store description).
  • Vostok: high risk and high reward; store text states that dying in Vostok costs your full progression context for that run, unlike losses limited to what you carry elsewhere.
  • Shelters: described as safe zones and the way to save; loot can be stored and furniture customized in decoration mode.
  • Traders: barter economy (no cash); themed gear, tasks and services including medical care and weapon maintenance (same store page). See also NPCs & traders.
  • Loot and UI: physics-based loot, tetris-style inventories, combining and stacking items. Item tables live under Wiki.

A separate Road To Vostok Demo is linked from Steam (AppID 2141300 on the same page). Public demos have included a tutorial map that introduces mechanics such as medical care, trading, shelter, and looting before the main sandbox — prioritize that before reading guides alone. Afterward, use Guides for written walkthroughs.
gettingStartedPage.sectionControlsHtml:
Basic controls & how to move (PC)

Default bindings change between builds. Use Settings → Controls in your installed copy as the source of truth. The table below is a reference layout aggregated from a third-party PC controls write-up (Magic Game World, first published 2021, since revised) so you know what to look for — not a guarantee for your patch. Weapon UI details also appear in the weapon loading section on this page.

Default-style PC controls for Road To Vostok (reference)
CategoryActionTypical binding
MovementMoveW A S D
Run / sprintShift / Ctrl (per guide)
JumpSpace
CrouchC
LeanQ / E
LadderMiddle mouse (per guide)
InteractMiddle mouse (per guide)
WeaponsFire / aimLMB / RMB
Primary / secondary1 / 2
ReloadR
Inspect weaponX (per guide)
Fire modeF
Low / high ready (scroll)Mouse wheel
InventoryGrab itemG
Move / drop / rotate itemLMB / Shift+LMB / R (context)
Main UITab

The same guide lists extra weapon-UI shortcuts (attachments, hiding UI elements, optic adjustments while aiming). If a key does nothing, your build may have remapped it — check the in-game binding screen.
gettingStartedPage.sectionWeaponsHtml:
Road To Vostok: how to load shotgun, weapons & magazines

Magazine-fed weapons

Steam community discussions often explain that R is not a magic “fill gun” key: you need magazines loaded with cartridges in your kit. A common player-described flow is: remove a magazine, feed rounds into it in the inventory UI, re-seat the magazine, then use R to swap to a ready magazine during combat. Carrying a spare filled magazine is widely recommended so you can tap R under pressure. Details vary by weapon — see our weapon loading section and the Ammunition table for cartridge planning.

Shotguns, bolt-actions, and other non-magazine guns

These weapons use manual, per-round loading. In Steam discussion threads about shotgun loading (late 2024), several players state that the in-game tutorial explicitly demonstrates the sequence for shotgun and sniper-style weapons (one reply: pick tutorial weapons and ammo, then follow on-screen steps).

Because keybindings differ by layout and patch, treat the following only as examples of what players reported in that thread — not universal facts:

  • Hold Alt and use right mouse button to load (one commenter).
  • Hold Ctrl or right Alt and click left mouse repeatedly to feed shells (another commenter).
  • One player wrote that in their tutorial it was: press P, then load each round with LMB, and after firing use R to cycle the manual reload step — this may match a specific demo build only.

If you spawn with an empty chamber, you can be killed before you finish loading. Do the tutorial segment for these weapons first, then confirm the same keys under Settings → Controls in your build.
gettingStartedPage.sectionMedicalHtml:
Survival & medical — bleeding, fractures, healing

Steam’s feature list positions medical supplies alongside food, tools, and weapons, and states traders can provide medical care as a service. For item-level numbers (heal amount, use time, which affliction each item clears), common reference points come from the community-run Road To Vostok wiki on Fandom (Medical and Afflictions articles). Those values can change with patches — verify against your in-game tooltips. For weapon planning use Weapons; for mined borders and regional context see Minefield tactics and border geography.

Fractures (“died from broken bone” situations)

Per the Fandom Afflictions page, a fracture causes ongoing health drain (listed as −1 health unit every 5 seconds in that article), plus heavy movement penalties (e.g. movement, swim, and jump reductions described there). Treat it as urgent: stabilize before you bleed stamina trying to sprint out.

The same wiki lists cures for fractures as Splint, Medkit, IFAK, and AFAK. The Medical table lists Splint as 4 seconds to apply, 0 direct health restored — it addresses the fracture condition rather than topping your HP bar by itself.

Bleeding and restoring health

Fandom’s affliction summary ties bleeding to faster health loss than a fracture and lists cures such as bandage, tourniquet, medkit, IFAK, and AFAK. The Medical item table (same wiki) lists example values such as Bandage — 4 s use, +20 health (and notes it addresses bleeding/burn in that row), Medkit / IFAK — 4 s, +50 health, with broader cure lists per item.

Long-term survival also depends on energy and hydration: the Afflictions page states that at 0 energy or 0 hydration you take passive health damage over time until you eat or drink appropriately — separate from gunshot fractures but just as lethal if ignored.

Selected medical items (community wiki, April 2025 snapshot — verify in-game)
ItemUse timeHealth (wiki)Notes (wiki)
Bandage4 s+20Bleeding / burn (per wiki table)
Tourniquet4 s0Bleeding focus
Splint4 s0Fracture
Medkit4 s+50Multiple severe afflictions per wiki
IFAK4 s+50Advanced field kit per wiki

Task-related loot is summarized under Core task items.
gettingStartedPage.sectionCraftingHtml:
How to craft in Road To Vostok

Many players search how to craft in Road To Vostok (including the common typo “crasft”). Recipes and workbenches expand across Early Access patches — use in-game UI and patch notes as the source of truth. Our Wiki lists loot that often feeds crafting loops: Gathering, Fishing when your build includes it, and Core task items for shelter cabling questions. Build announcements sit in Road ahead.

modArticlePage.commentsDisclosure: Short summaries for context—not verbatim Workshop posts.

modsListPage.title: Road To Vostok Mods
modsListPage.introP1Html: Here we publish editorial mod spotlights: packs and tools we think are worth reading about before you install—gameplay overhauls, QoL compilations, loader requirements (e.g. Metro Mod Loader), and what each listing changes in a run. The list below is our curated recommendations, not an exhaustive ModWorkshop index; we add entries as we review them.
modsListPage.introP2Html: We do not host .vmz files. Every card links to the author’s source (ModWorkshop or as stated) for the real download. Match the file to your demo or Early Access build and verify checksums before you load anything into the game.

posterPage.title: Road to Vostok poster & screenshot gallery
posterPage.leadHtml: This page is part of Road To Vostok Guide — our English survival reference for the game: interactive map, item & weapon database, tasks & progression, strategy guides, and roadmap context. Here we host a road to vostok poster-style gallery of in-game stills so you can browse atmosphere shots without leaving the guide. Click any image for a larger view.
posterPage.featureCapHtml: Featured road to vostok poster still — screenshot 1 of 12. Click to enlarge.
posterPage.galleryIntroHtml: Twelve frames in one place for quick browsing. Pair with the wiki for loadouts, the map for POIs, and guides when you want written walkthroughs — all on this site.
posterPage.footnoteHtml: Images © Road to Vostok Ltd. Source: roadtovostok.com/screenshots. Copied for guide context only; for press or commercial use follow the developer’s site and press kit terms.

aboutPage.title: About Road To Vostok Guide
aboutPage.lead: What this English-language guide is for, and what it is not.
aboutPage.purposeText: Road To Vostok Guide is a player-focused help site: maps and tasks, item tables, how-to guides for weapons and medical systems, save paths on PC, and summaries of public roadmap news. We aim for clear, searchable pages that complement the official game site and Steam store — not replace them.
aboutPage.independenceBeforeLinksHtml: We are not the official Road To Vostok team. We do not speak for the developer or publisher. Builds, loot, and mechanics change; always double-check critical details in your installed game and on
aboutPage.exploreHtml: Start with Start here for controls and first steps, Wiki for tables, and Map / Tasks for progression help. Updates and build names are summarized under Road ahead.

contactPage.title: Road To Vostok — Contact Us
contactPage.lead: Reach the Road To Vostok Guide site by email. We do not use a contact form on this page.
contactPage.emailIntroHtml: For corrections to guides, broken links, copyright concerns, or general questions about this website, write to:
contactPage.emailFollowHtml: Please include a clear subject line and, when reporting a content issue, the page URL and a short description of what should be fixed. We are a small editorial team; replies may take several business days.
contactPage.gameSupportHtml: For bugs, purchases, or account issues with Road To Vostok itself, contact the game’s official support channels (for example via Steam or the developer’s site). We cannot reset saves, refund purchases, or change game builds.
contactPage.privacyHtml: If your message contains personal data, see our Privacy Policy for how we handle correspondence.

notFoundPage.title: Road To Vostok — Page not found
notFoundPage.lead: This URL is not part of Road To Vostok Guide. Use the navigation or return home.

copyrightPage.title: Road To Vostok — Copyright
copyrightPage.lead: How original site content and third-party game materials are credited on Road To Vostok Guide.
copyrightPage.gameHtml: Road To Vostok and related logos, artwork, and game content are the property of their respective rights holders. This site is not affiliated with those rights holders. All game-related trademarks are used here nominatively to identify the game for readers and players.
copyrightPage.siteHtml: Original text, layout, and compilation on roadtovostok.org (unless otherwise noted) are copyright © the operators of Road To Vostok Guide, except where content is clearly quoted or attributed to community or official sources. Editorial guides and tables may incorporate factual gameplay information; that does not imply ownership of the underlying game assets or IP.
copyrightPage.takedownHtml: If you are a rights holder and believe material on this site infringes your copyright, contact us with a clear description of the work and URL. We will review good-faith requests promptly. Email:

privacyPolicyPage.title: Road To Vostok — Privacy Policy
privacyPolicyPage.lead: How Road To Vostok Guide (roadtovostok.org) handles information when you browse this site.
privacyPolicyPage.whoLead: This website is an independent gaming reference for Road To Vostok. It is not operated by the game’s developer or publisher. For questions about this policy, contact
privacyPolicyPage.collectP1Html: Like most websites, our hosting or infrastructure may automatically process technical data when you visit, such as your IP address, browser type, approximate region, timestamps, and pages requested. We do not use this information to identify you personally unless required for security or legal compliance.
privacyPolicyPage.collectP2Html: If you email us, we receive your email address and the contents of your message so we can respond. We do not sell your email or use it for marketing unless you explicitly agree.
privacyPolicyPage.cookiesHtml: The site may use cookies or similar technologies that are strictly necessary for basic operation (for example, session or preference storage in your browser). We do not run third-party advertising cookies on these pages as part of this policy’s scope. If we add analytics or embedded media that set their own cookies, we will update this policy and, where required, your consent flow.
privacyPolicyPage.thirdPartyHtml: Pages may link to external sites (for example, the official game site, Steam, or YouTube). Those services have their own privacy practices; we are not responsible for their policies or data use.
privacyPolicyPage.childrenHtml: This site is a general-audience game guide. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has sent us personal data, contact us at the address above and we will delete it where appropriate.
privacyPolicyPage.rightsHtml: Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, or delete personal data we hold about you (for example, email correspondence). Contact us using the email above and we will respond within a reasonable time.
privacyPolicyPage.changesHtml: We may update this Privacy Policy when the site or legal requirements change. The “Last updated” date at the top will be revised when we do. Continued use of the site after changes means you accept the updated policy.

termsOfServicePage.title: Road To Vostok — Terms of Service
termsOfServicePage.lead: Rules for using Road To Vostok Guide (roadtovostok.org). By using the site, you agree to these terms.
termsOfServicePage.aboutHtml: Road To Vostok Guide is an independent reference site. It is not endorsed by, affiliated with, or sponsored by the developers or publishers of Road To Vostok or any related trademark holders. Game names and marks belong to their respective owners.
termsOfServicePage.useHtml: Guides, tables, and text are provided for personal, non-commercial reference to help players. You may link to our pages. Do not scrape or redistribute large portions of the site in a way that competes with or replaces the live site without permission. Game data and numbers may be inaccurate or outdated; always verify against your installed build and official sources.
termsOfServicePage.warrantyHtml: The site is provided “as is.” We make no guarantee of accuracy, availability, or fitness for a particular purpose. We are not liable for any loss or damage arising from your use of the site or reliance on its content.
termsOfServicePage.conductHtml: Do not use the site to harass others, distribute malware, attempt unauthorized access to our systems, or break applicable laws. We may restrict access if we reasonably believe these terms are violated.
termsOfServicePage.changesHtml: We may change these Terms of Service at any time. We will update the “Last updated” date when we do. Your continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
